beats.narration_text: Bonjour. A learner asks: build me an interactive simulation of rabi oscillations vs perturbation theory. One Claude prompt. Here is what comes back.

props.command: build me an interactive sim of rabi oscillations vs perturbation theory

beats.narration_text:
Rabi Oscillations vs Perturbation Theory is one of those ideas that is easier to see than to state. Exact Rabi formula (RED) versus first-order perturbation theory (SLATE dashed).
  At resonance (δ/Ω = 0), PT diverges — it predicts probability > 100%.. This simulation makes that visible — and every educator can build one.
beats.new_visual_element: Concept card: Rabi Oscillations vs Perturbation Theory

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Concept card: Rabi Oscillations vs Perturbation Theory

beats.narration_text: Here is the prompt. One message to Claude.
beats.code_snippet:
Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Rabi Oscillations vs Perturbation Theory. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: δ/Ω =. Verify: Exact Rabi formula (RED) versus first-order perturbation theory (SLATE dashed).
  At resonance (δ/Ω = 0), PT diverges — . Palette: background #FFFFFF, ink #2A1A0E, accent #C8102E.

props.command: Build a single-file interactive HTML simulation of Rabi Oscillations vs Perturbation Theory. Use D3.js from CDN. All physics computed in-browser JavaScript. Sliders for: δ/Ω =. Verify: Exact Rabi form

beats.narration_text: Claude returns a single-file HTML. Here is the heart of it — the integrator and the slider handler. No server. No build step. One file, everything in the browser.

Now drag δ/Ω = from 0 to 3. Watch the physics respond in real time.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Slate: narrate slider sweep of δ/Ω = from 0 to 3

beats.narration_text:
Exact Rabi formula (RED) versus first-order perturbation theory (SLATE dashed).
  At resonance (δ/Ω = 0), PT diverges — it predicts probability > 100%.. Exactly as the equations predict.

One prompt. A live demonstration of rabi oscillations vs perturbation theory. For educators: every concept in your course can have one of these. Claude builds it; you judge it; your students explore it.
Open this file in your browser and drag the controls. Or ask Claude to add a second parameter, change the palette, or explain the physics step by step alongside the simulation.
Take this prompt, run it on your own topic. Your turn.
beats.code_snippet: Take this Rabi Oscillations vs Perturbation Theory simulation and add a second slider that controls δ/Ω =. Show how changing it reveals a new aspect of the physics. Keep the single-file format and D3 CDN.
